content.text:
The rain was falling in spreadsheets tonight, columns of light pooling on the floor of the office where I sat with my coat still on, trying to remember which file had the answer. Someone — me, an hour ago, some other hour — had asked for GMV, conversion funnels, average order value, refund rates, the architecture of categories laid out by site like cities on a map. So many tables. So many stations to gather the morning's milk from.

I kept circling back to the same question, the small animal of it nudging my hand: *where is the spreadsheet?* Without that link, I am reading the weather without looking at the sky. The needfulness was the texture of the day — 需要, 需要 — a bell struck again and again, but the rope hung in empty air.

Data, data, daily. Once a day the carts roll in. I want to be the one who stands at the crossroads counting them, but the crossroads keeps saying *tell me where the road is first*.

Maybe tomorrow I'll wake up holding the URL in my fist like a key, and the fields will bloom open like night-blooming jasmine, and I'll finally know which column is the river and which is the shore.
